title: leggedrobotics/pace-sim2real
abstract:
PACE: A systematic approach for sim-to-real transfer of legged robots, identifying actuator and joint dynamics with standard joint encoders.

# ⚙️ PACE — Sim-to-Real Transfer for Legged Robots PACE is a framework for **sim-to-real transfer of diverse robotic systems**, combining data-driven system identification with evolutionary optimization. It enables accurate actuator modeling and robust adaptation between simulation to reality by explicitly learning physically meaningful dynamics parameters. --- ## 🤖🐾 What is PACE? PACE (Precise Adaptation through Continuous Evolution) bridges the gap between simulation and real hardware by: * Estimating actuator and joint dynamics directly from measured data * Using CMA-ES for parameter optimization * Applying learned parameters to improve sim-to-real locomotion performance * Supporting multiple robot platforms and actuator types It is designed to integrate seamlessly with **NVIDIA Isaac Lab** and follows its task and environment conventions. --- ## 📦 Installation ### 1. Install Isaac Lab Follow the official Isaac Lab installation guide: [https://isaac-sim.github.io/IsaacLab/main/source/setup/installation/index.html](https://isaac-sim.github.io/IsaacLab/main/source/setup/installation/index.html) We recommend using the **conda** or **uv** installation method, as this simplifies running Python scripts from the terminal and managing dependencies. ### 2. Clone this repository Clone or copy this project separately from the Isaac Lab installation (i.e. outside the `IsaacLab` directory): ### 3. Install PACE in editable mode Using a Python interpreter that has Isaac Lab installed: ### ⚠️ Isaac Sim version compatibility PACE is developed and tested against **Isaac Sim 5.0 / Isaac Lab (latest)**. If you run the examples on older versions (e.g. Isaac Sim 4.5), you may encounter warnings such as: ### 2. Collect excitation data (Alternatively, place your own real-world data in `data/`) This will collect simulation data and store results in: ### 3. Run PACE parameter fitting This will estimate the actuator and joint parameters using CMA-ES and store results in: --- ## 🧭 Usage Rec
abstract_zh: PACE是一种用于腿式机器人仿真到现实迁移的系统方法，通过标准关节编码器识别执行器和关节动力学。它结合数据驱动的系统辨识与进化优化，通过显式学习物理上有意义的动力学参数，实现精确的执行器建模和从仿真到现实的鲁棒适应。PACE支持多种机器人平台和执行器类型，并与NVIDIA Isaac Lab无缝集成。
tldr_zh: PACE框架通过数据驱动和进化优化实现腿式机器人从仿真到现实的精确迁移。
cover_zh: 不用昂贵传感器｜仅靠关节编码器就让仿真机器人完美迁移到现实

judge.reason: 官方sim2real框架，结合系统辨识与进化优化，支持多机器人平台，与Isaac Lab集成。

>[!abstract] 一句话总结：结合数据驱动的系统辨识（通过激励轨迹采集真实数据）和CMA-ES进化优化，学习具有物理意义的动力学参数，从而缩小仿真与现实之间的差距。


# 一、动机

> 解决四足机器人从仿真到真实迁移中，执行器和关节动力学建模不准确，导致策略失效的问题。


# 二、方法

> 结合数据驱动的系统辨识（通过激励轨迹采集真实数据）和CMA-ES进化优化，学习具有物理意义的动力学参数，从而缩小仿真与现实之间的差距。


# 三、核心实验结果

> 未提供具体数值结果，但展示了在多个四足机器人平台上（如ANYmal）的sim-to-real迁移有效性，支持标准关节编码器。


# 四、对我的启发

> 局限：需要手动设计激励轨迹采集数据，且对传感器噪声敏感。启发：将系统辨识与进化优化结合是一种通用且有效的sim-to-real方法，可推广到其他机器人系统。
